_description: Independent verification of Phase 1 results after autoresearch sweep. Re-trained winning config from scratch.

t1b_sequence_auroc.note: Was 0.663 at 80 epochs → 0.9994 at 500 epochs. Undertraining was the bottleneck.

t2_score_gradient.note: Distance to real data INCREASES 12% during Langevin sampling. Score gradient does NOT guide toward data manifold. Unchanged from baseline.

t3_sampling_near_data.note: 0% of samples land within distance 1.0 of real data. Samples are diverse (std=1.42) but not on the manifold.

key_finding: FF CAN learn sequence discrimination (T1b PASS at 500 epochs), BUT the learned energy landscape does NOT produce a useful score gradient for diffusion sampling (T2/T3 FAIL). The energy separates data/noise by MAGNITUDE but the GRADIENT does not point toward data. This is a fundamental limitation of FF's local goodness objective for score-based generation: what makes a good discriminator does not necessarily make a good generative model.
verdict: PARTIAL: FF learns discriminative energy but not generative energy. The diffusion pipeline cannot work as-is because the score gradient is adversarial to sampling. Further work would require a different training objective (score matching rather than goodness separation) or a different sampling scheme.
